translations.notes: is_htmx(request: HttpRequest) → isHtmx(headers: IncomingHttpHeaders). The function signature changes from a full Django request to the headers map only — more generic and testable without a framework mock. The header name is lowercased ('hx-request') because Node.js normalises all incoming HTTP header names to lowercase, whereas Django's HttpRequest.headers is case-insensitive. Behaviour is identical: returns true iff the header value is exactly the string 'true'. The function is retained even though HTMX disappears in the SPA — it may be useful during the Strangler-Fig migration window.

Only clean_integrity_error() is migrated → parseSequelizeError(). The two dead-code symbols (ResourceNotFound, get_or_404) are intentionally excluded — see flagged_for_review. DESIGN: the Python source does manual string-splitting on raw psycopg2/sqlite3 error messages. Sequelize already parses the constraint violation and exposes the field name as error.errors[0].path, making the translation considerably simpler while preserving the same public contract: returns a field name string or null. The catch-all try/catch is preserved to match the silent-failure behaviour of the source.

Django TestCase (unittest) → Jest describe/it. The four test cases are translated 1-to-1. Fixtures change: instead of constructing a django.db.IntegrityError with __cause__ set to a raw sqlite3.IntegrityError, we construct Sequelize UniqueConstraintError objects directly using ValidationErrorItem, which is the idiomatic Jest/Sequelize equivalent. The malformed-message test (test_returns_none_for_malformed_message) becomes 'empty errors array' — Sequelize handles message parsing internally so there is no equivalent 'malformed raw message'; an empty errors[] is the nearest analog of a constraint error that cannot be field-resolved.

not_migrated.reason: Django context processor (SSR rendering concern). In the SPA architecture this mechanism does not exist: Angular's AuthService/AuthStore exposes currentUser$ reactively and is injected wherever needed. The user data fields (username, email, bio, image) and the bio/image empty-string→null normalisation (edge case of BR-003) must be implemented in the Angular AuthService during the templates/accounts migration sprints.

Confirmed dead code by Phase 0 cartography (agent 01). No callers found in any module. Django utility wrappers (get_object_or_404, Http404) have no equivalent role in Fastify — routes use Sequelize findOne() directly and return reply.code(404) inline.
not_migrated.action_required: None — intentionally excluded. Validation checkpoint HV-001 (Phase 3) documents this decision.

flagged_for_review.reason: isHtmx() is retained as a utility but has no callers in the current target codebase. Its only future use would be in Strangler-Fig proxying adapters. If those adapters are not implemented, this file can be deleted without impact.
flagged_for_review.suggestion: After completing the migration of all HTMX endpoints (articles, accounts, comments sprints), verify whether isHtmx() has any callers. Remove the file if unused at Phase 5 audit.

flagged_for_review.reason: parseSequelizeError relies on error.errors[0].path being populated by Sequelize. This is always the case for UniqueConstraintError raised by Sequelize itself, but may not hold if the error is constructed manually or comes from a raw Sequelize.query() call that bypasses the ORM layer.
flagged_for_review.suggestion: Verify with integration tests (accounts + articles sprints) that UniqueConstraintError.errors[0].path is set correctly for both sqlite (local) and PostgreSQL (production) backends.
